etymology.description: 糹(糹) (sī, "silk; thread") + 及(及) (jí, "reach") = thread-related sequence -> level; grade
etymology.interpretation: The character 糹(糹) (sī, "silk; thread") signifies a sequence or order related to silk, while 及(及) (jí, "reach") provides the sound. The idea is of arranging threads in a sequence, symbolizing structured levels or grades.
etymology.simplification: 級(級) was simplified to 级(级) by replacing 糹(糹) (sī, "silk; thread") with the simplified form 纟(纟), reducing the number of strokes.

examples.chinese: 她的数学水平达到了一定的级别(她的數學水平達到了一定的級別)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de shùxué shuǐpíng dádào le yīdìng de jíbié.
examples.english: Her math skills have reached a certain level.

examples.chinese: 每个级别都有不同的挑战(每個級別都有不同的挑戰)。
examples.pinyin: Měi gè jíbié dōu yǒu bùtóng de tiǎozhàn.

examples.chinese: 他在比赛中表现出了高级的技能(他在比賽中表現出了高級的技能)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zài bǐsài zhōng biǎoxiàn chūle gāojí de jìnéng.
examples.english: He demonstrated advanced skills in the competition.
